description: Add Telegram bot command to import existing Git repositories into ralph via SSH URL, cloning repos and creating minimal prd.json configuration

userStories.title: Configure projects directory path
userStories.description: As a bot administrator, I want to configure where imported repositories are stored so that imported projects are organized correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Configuration option for projectsDirectory path
Default to ./ralph-projects if not configured
Bot creates directory if it doesn't exist
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add import command handler to Telegram bot
userStories.description: As a user, I want to use an /import command so I can start importing a repository.
acceptanceCriteria: Bot responds to /import command
Bot prompts for Git SSH URL after command is issued
Bot handles invalid URL format with helpful error message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Handle duplicate project names
userStories.description: As a user, when importing a repository with a name that already exists, I want to be informed clearly so I can take corrective action.
acceptanceCriteria: Bot checks if project directory already exists before cloning
If exists, bot sends clear error message: A project with this name already exists. Please use a different repository or rename first.
Bot does NOT create partial duplicate directory
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Clone Git repository from SSH URL
userStories.description: As a user, when I provide a valid Git SSH URL, I want the bot to clone the repository into the ralph projects directory.
acceptanceCriteria: Bot executes git clone with provided SSH URL
Repository cloned to configured projects directory
Clone progress shown to user via bot messages
Handles authentication failures with clear error
Timeout after 5 minutes for large repos
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create minimal prd.json file
userStories.description: As a user, I want the bot to create a minimal prd.json file so ralph detects the imported project.
acceptanceCriteria: prd.json created in cloned repo root directory
Contains name field derived from repo name
Contains repoUrl field the SSH URL provided
Contains importedAt field ISO timestamp
Valid JSON format
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate cloned repository
userStories.description: As a user, I want the bot to validate the cloned repository before marking it as successfully imported so I know the import is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify .git directory exists valid git repo
Warn user if package.json, Cargo.toml, or other common dependency files are missing
Send success message with project name and path after import
Typecheck passes
